verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass integral membrane protein that displays documented plasma membrane residence via its own vesicular trafficking mechanisms (VTC-mediated delivery, endocytic recycling). Surface presence is confirmed by cell surface biotinylation, TIRF microscopy, and pH-sensitive pHluorin assays on intact cells. While ATG9A is canonically resident at the trans-Golgi network, early/recycling endosomes, and autophagosomal structures (intracellular baseline), a documented minority pool reaches the plasma membrane under baseline conditions and is significantly enriched upon cell stress (PM damage, starvation). This qualifies as dual_localization: the protein maintains its own TM-anchored topology at the PM, enters through vesicular trafficking from intracellular compartments, and is retained via endocytic retrieval. The surface pool is real, functional (PM integrity repair), and accessible via the extracellular face.
